steps.title: Graph both sides first
steps.content:
This is an absolute-value equation, so the quickest check is to graph the two sides in Desmos:
$$y=-2|x-7|$$
$$y=-8x$$
The solution is the $x$-coordinate where the graphs intersect.

steps.title: Confirm the one intersection
steps.content:
The graphs intersect only once, at $x=\frac{7}{5}$. Algebraically, dividing the original equation by $-2$ gives $|x-7|=4x$, so $x$ must be nonnegative. For the valid branch $x<7$,
$$7-x=4x$$
$$x=\frac{7}{5}$$
The negative value in choices A and C fails the nonnegative-side check, and choice D includes an extra value. Choice B is correct.
